sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The player pressed SPACE and moved down twice. Let me check if the left square was collected.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'c', 'W', 'G', 'b']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)}), children={n['children']}")

# Check ascii
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for i in range(28, 42):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][14:28] if len(ascii_lines[i]) > 28 else ascii_lines[i]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The left square is gone! Looking at the state:
- id=5: b at (28,28)-(31,39) - center blue bar (still there)
- id=7: W at (36,16)-(40,19) - white region with blue center
- id=8: b at (37,17)-(38,18) - blue center inside white region
- id=9: N at (41,16)-(43,19) - player
The left square (which was at rows 28-31, cols 16-19) is gone. Instead, there's a white region with blue center at rows 36-40, cols 16-19. This looks like the left square was "collected" and moved down to be carried by the player!
So the collection mechanic is:
1. Player approaches square → square turns G
2. Player presses SPACE → white strip extends to cover square
3. Player moves away → square is "collected" and moves to follow the player (becoming a white-framed object with blue center)
Now I need to collect the right square and the center bar. Let me first check if the center bar is still the same.
Looking at the ascii:
- Row 28: wwwwwwwwwwwwww (all white - left square is gone)
- Row 29-31: wwwwwwwwwwwwww (all white)
- The center bar is at rows 28-31, cols 28-39
So the left square is collected! Now I need to collect the right square and the center bar. Let me approach the right square next.
Right square is at (24,44)-(27,47). Player is at (41,16)-(43,19). I need to move RIGHT and UP to get to the right square.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Left square collected! Now collect the right square.
# Right square is at (24,44)-(27,47). Player is at (41,16)-(43,19).
# Need to move RIGHT to align cols (16-19 -> 44-47), that's about 28 cols right
# Each RIGHT moves 4 cols, so RIGHT 7 moves 28 cols
# Also need to move UP to get closer to row 24-27
# Player at rows 41-43, need to get to ~rows 32-35 to trigger G
# UP moves 4 rows each, so UP 2 gets to rows 33-35
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
